mnemonics.title: Viewing Pipeline Order
mnemonics.content: My Very Petunia Needs Dandelions - Modeling → Viewing → Projection → Normalization → Device transformation

mnemonics.title: UVN Camera Vectors
mnemonics.content: UP Never - U (right), V (up), N (normal/view direction)

mnemonics.title: Cohen-Sutherland Quick Test
mnemonics.content:
Trivial Accept: Both endpoints inside (0000)
Trivial Reject: AND of outcodes ≠ 0

acronyms.expansion: Up Vector, View-up Vector, Normal Vector (camera coordinate system)
acronyms.usage: Defines orientation of viewing coordinate system relative to world coordinates
Device-independent coordinate system (-1 to 1) used between viewing and device transformation
Angular extent of the scene visible to the camera, affects perspective distortion
Basic 2D elements like points, lines, polygons that undergo viewing transformations

visualTips.visualization: Think of a photograph (window) being scanned onto a specific frame (viewport). The entire photo shrinks or stretches to fit the frame while maintaining relative positions.
Imagine a flashlight beam (frustum) - objects inside the cone are visible, those outside are in darkness. Near plane is the flashlight opening, far plane is where light fades.
Orthographic is like architectural blueprints (parallel lines stay parallel). Perspective is like human vision or photography (parallel lines converge at horizon).

analogies.analogy: Like taking a photo: position camera (viewing), choose lens (projection), decide print size (window-viewport), develop on paper (device).
Like looking through a car window - you only see what's within the frame; everything outside your peripheral view is ignored.
Think of a TV screen as the viewport - no matter what movie (world) you play, it fits within the screen's aspect ratio.
The UVN system is like holding a smartphone - U is right/left, V is up/down thumb direction, N is where the camera lens points.
